steps.title: Find the slope between two data points
Use point-slope form to find the equation
steps.content:
With point $(-s, 21)$ and slope $-\frac{3}{s}$, apply point-slope form:
$$y - 21 = -\frac{3}{s}(x + s)$$

Multiply both sides by $s$:
$$s(y - 21) = -3(x + s)$$
$$sy - 21s = -3x - 3s$$
$$3x + sy = 18s$$

Verify with all three points: $3(-2s) + s(24) = 18s$ ✓, $3(-s) + s(21) = 18s$ ✓, $3(s) + s(15) = 18s$ ✓

That matches choice B — B is correct.
